questiontext.text:
For this question, you need to create a function void print_array(int *ptr, int len) which takes a pointer to an array of integers and prints each of them separated by commas and spaces. To help with this, it also takes in the length of the array as a parameter named len. I know it seems easy (I was going to set a harder challenge but Fred said I wasn't allowed to). 

There is a twist though! We had a word with IT, and it turns out they're running very low on square brackets. In order to make sure there's enough for you to use in your other classes, we've decided to ban them from all the functions you write in this exercise, so you'll have to do everything with just pointers. You can still use them in any main functions you write to test your functions, so long as you use them responsibly. (Don't worry though, some more are due to be delivered next week). 

Say we have an array, and we call the function on it like so:

int completely_random_nums[14] = {99, 97, 114, 97, 109, 101, 108, 32, 119, 97, 102, 101, 114, 0}

print_array(nums, 14);

Will result in the following output:

99, 97, 114, 97, 109, 101, 108, 32, 119, 97, 102, 101, 114, 0

Paste in your function when you think it's working
